chapter_context.summary: The 'Love Chapter,' explaining that without agape love, all spiritual gifts and knowledge are meaningless.

outline.explanation: This section focuses on The Necessity of Love (13:1-3), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 1 Corinthians.
This section focuses on The Characteristics of Love (13:4-7), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 1 Corinthians.
This section focuses on The Permanence of Love (13:8-13), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 1 Corinthians.

1.commentary:
### Tyndale Study Notes
1 Corinthians 13:1: The 'Hymn to Love' defines Agape not as an emotion but as a choice of the will. It is the 'more excellent way' compared to charismatic gifts.
1.full_commentary:
### Matthew Henry Commentary
We see here the great necessity of humility in the Christian walk. Love is the king of graces. It is better to love than to speak with tongues of angels. It is the very nature of God manifested in His people.

### Jamieson-Fausset-Brown
Analysis of the Greek text reveals Paul's rhetorical strategy. The term 'wisdom' (sophia) here refers to the speculative philosophy highly prized in Corinthian culture, which Paul subverts with the kerygma of the Cross.

key_themes.title: Unity in Christ
key_themes.description: Overcoming divisions and factions within the church.

key_themes.title: Christian Love
key_themes.description: The necessity and characteristics of agape love.

key_themes.title: The Resurrection
key_themes.description: The historical and theological reality of Christ's resurrection and our own.

devotional.question: How does this passage speak to your local church community today?

cultural_historical.title: Corinthian Culture
cultural_historical.text: Corinth was a major Greek port city known for its wealth, diversity, and moral challenges, all of which influenced the church's struggles.
